channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Quarterfinal Fixtures Announced
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has advanced to its quarterfinal stage, with the last-eight matches set to take place across multiple locations. The fixtures, listed in South Africa time zones, include key en...

item.title: Argentina Advances to World Cup Final Against Spain After Dramatic Comeback
item.description: Argentina delivered a stunning late comeback to defeat England 2-1 in a thrilling FIFA 2026 World Cup semi-final in Atlanta, securing their place in the final against Spain. The defending champions lo...

item.title: Tumbatu farmers face agricultural challenges due to climate and pests
item.description: Farmers on Tumbatu Islet in Zanzibar’s Unguja North Region are struggling with significant agricultural losses caused by monkey invasions, crop pests, unpredictable climate patterns, and limited farmi...

item.title: FBI Expands Probe into Qatar-Gifted Air Force One Security Vulnerabilities Involving Secret Service Agents
item.description: The FBI is intensifying its investigation into how security vulnerabilities of President Donald Trump's Qatar-gifted Air Force One jet were leaked to The New York Times. Secret Service agents who acco...

item.title: Peterborough City Council Sells Hindu Temple Complex to Islamic Charity, Promises Alternative Premises
item.description: A dispute has emerged over the Peterborough City Council's decision to sell a community complex housing a Hindu temple since 1986 to the United Kingdom Islamic Mission (UKIM). The temple, established ...

item.title: Alberta Experiences Record Rainfall After Wildfire Seasons
item.description: Alberta is facing unprecedented rainfall this summer, marking a stark contrast to the wildfire smoke that typically dominates the region. Edmonton, in particular, has been hit by record-breaking storm...

item.title: Norse Scientists Develop Fiber-Optic Method to Track Silent Marine Animals and Ships
item.description: Scientists from the Norwegian University of Science and Technology have developed a groundbreaking method using seabed fiber-optic cables to track silent underwater objects, including whales and ships...

item.title: Jesy Nelson Announces Expansion of Screening Tests Across the UK in Her SMA Campaign
item.description: Jesy Nelson, a British singer and former member of the girl group Sugababes, has celebrated a significant milestone in her advocacy for Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) awareness. The singer confirmed th...

item.title: New Zealand's conservation law reforms face backlash over potential environmental and Māori rights impacts
item.description: The New Zealand government's proposed reforms to nature protection laws have sparked fierce opposition from environmental groups and the outdoor recreation sector. Key changes include reassigning the ...

item.title: Albanese unveils AI Office and national framework for AI governance
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced the establishment of a new Office of AI and a national framework titled 'Australian Standards' to address challenges posed by artificial intelligence. The...

item.title: Understanding Humidex: How It Measures Heat and Comfort in Canada
item.description: The article explains the concept of humidex, a weather index used in Canada to describe how hot or cold it feels based on temperature and humidity levels. Developed by Canadian meteorologists, humidex...

item.title: Anthony Redpath's 'Chance at Bliss' Exhibition Captures Vancouver Summer Magic
item.description: Anthony Redpath’s latest exhibition, 'Chance at Bliss,' opens July 18 at Bau-Xi Vancouver, offering a cinematic celebration of Vancouver’s summer essence. Through meticulously combined photographs, Re...

item.title: Tanzania's DITF boosts regional trade with record sales and local manufacturing growth
item.description: The 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) concluded with significant achievements, generating 3.89 billion shillings in on-site sales and 1.2 billion shillings in advance export orders. T...

item.title: Understanding Stress-Induced Exhaustion and Mental Overdrive
item.description: The article explores the paradox of feeling physically exhausted yet mentally alert due to chronic stress. It explains how modern stressors like work pressures, social media, and constant connectivity...

item.title: Marumo Gallants support player amid police investigation into alleged fake kidnapping
item.description: Club owner Abram Sello confirmed that midfielder Thapelo Dhludhlu is cooperating with authorities following allegations he faked his kidnapping. The incident occurred when Dhludhlu disappeared from hi...

item.title: SWS Survey: Filipinos Favor Strengthening AFP and PCG Over New Defense Agreements
item.description: A recent Social Weather Stations (SWS) survey commissioned by the Stratbase Institute reveals that 45% of Filipinos prefer modernizing the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and Philippine Coast Gu...

item.title: US military halts tanker en route to Iranian port amid fresh strikes
item.description: The US military has disabled an unladen oil tanker attempting to sail towards an Iranian port, marking a significant escalation in regional tensions. This action follows a third wave of strikes launch...

item.title: Tinubu highlights Nigeria’s economic growth and collaboration with Deloitte
item.description: President Bola Tinubu recently praised Nigeria's economic progress during a meeting with Deloitte Africa, emphasizing that reforms over three years have stabilized the economy. He acknowledged Deloitt...

item.title: Suede Release 30th Anniversary Reissue of 'Coming Up' and 12-Inch Singles Collection
item.description: Suede have announced a 30th-anniversary reissue of their landmark 1996 album 'Coming Up', alongside a 12-inch singles collection. The reissue, released via Demon Music on September 25, includes restor...

item.title: Soyuz MS-29 Successfully Launches and DocksWith the ISS in 2026
item.description: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched on July 14, 2026, at 14:47 UTC from Baikonur Cosmodrome's PU-6 ramp, marking the first Soyuz launch of the year and the 75th mission to the Internation...
